6a2cbf315fef949429e44bf8.leadin: This singer co-founded the Aldeburgh Festival, where he starred as Aschenbach in the world premiere of the opera Death in Venice. For 10 points each:
6a2cbf315fef949429e44bf8.leadin_sanitized: This singer co-founded the Aldeburgh Festival, where he starred as Aschenbach in the world premiere of the opera Death in Venice. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this tenor who premiered the role of a fisherman who sings “Now the Great Bear and the Pleiades.”
For almost 40 years, Peter Pears was the romantic and professional partner of this English composer of Peter Grimes and The Young Person’s Guide to the Orchestra.

Together, Pears and Britten adapted this writer’s work into a 1960 opera that starred the countertenor Alfred Deller. Arrigo Boito (“BOH-ee-toh”) adapted this writer’s work into the libretti of Verdi’s final two operas.
parts_sanitized: Name this tenor who premiered the role of a fisherman who sings "Now the Great Bear and the Pleiades."
For almost 40 years, Peter Pears was the romantic and professional partner of this English composer of Peter Grimes and The Young Person's Guide to the Orchestra.

Together, Pears and Britten adapted this writer's work into a 1960 opera that starred the countertenor Alfred Deller. Arrigo Boito ("BOH-ee-toh") adapted this writer's work into the libretti of Verdi's final two operas.
answers: William Shakespeare (The Britten opera is A Midsummer Night’s Dream. Verdi’s operas are Otello and Falstaff.)
answers_sanitized: Peter Pears("peers") [or Sir Peter Neville Luard Pears]
William Shakespeare (The Britten opera is A Midsummer Night's Dream. Verdi's operas are Otello and Falstaff.)

6a2cbf315fef949429e44c10.question: A “secret” one of these events titles Domenico Cimarosa’s (“chee-mah-ROH-zah’s”) most famous opera. During one of these events, a baritone character opens a fake love letter that arranges a tryst “under the pines in the grove”; that letter was written by two sopranos in the duet “Sull’aria.” After one of these events, Lucia kills Arturo and enters the Great Hall soaked in blood, beginning her “mad scene.” After learning the title character is their long-lost son, (*) Marcellina (“mar-cheh-LEE-nah”) and Bartolo (“BAR-toh-loh”) participate in a “double” one of these events. In an opera titled for this kind of event, the Countess disguises herself as her maid Susanna to trick the unfaithful Count Almaviva. For 10 points, name this kind of event that titles a Mozart opera about Figaro.
6a2cbf315fef949429e44c10.question_sanitized: A "secret" one of these events titles Domenico Cimarosa's ("chee-mah-ROH-zah's") most famous opera. During one of these events, a baritone character opens a fake love letter that arranges a tryst "under the pines in the grove"; that letter was written by two sopranos in the duet "Sull'aria." After one of these events, Lucia kills Arturo and enters the Great Hall soaked in blood, beginning her "mad scene." After learning the title character is their long-lost son, (*) Marcellina ("mar-cheh-LEE-nah") and Bartolo ("BAR-toh-loh") participate in a "double" one of these events. In an opera titled for this kind of event, the Countess disguises herself as her maid Susanna to trick the unfaithful Count Almaviva. For 10 points, name this kind of event that titles a Mozart opera about Figaro.
6a2cbf315fef949429e44c10.answer: weddings [accept marriages or signing a marriage contract; accept The Marriage of Figaro or Le nozze di Figaro; accept The Secret Marriage or Il matrimonio segreto; prompt on signing a contract with “For what purpose?”]
6a2cbf315fef949429e44c10.answer_sanitized: weddings [accept marriages or signing a marriage contract; accept The Marriage of Figaro or Le nozze di Figaro; accept The Secret Marriage or Il matrimonio segreto; prompt on signing a contract with "For what purpose?"]

parts: Name this opera that was originally completed by Franco Alfano. In this Puccini opera, Calaf answers three riddles to win the hand of the title Chinese princess.
Puccini left Turandot unfinished after this secondary character’s funeral procession. This slave-girl secretly loves Calaf and tearfully begs him not to risk his life in the aria “Signore, ascolta!”
Before Liù’s death, Calaf expresses confidence that Turandot won’t learn his name in this aria. This aria ends with three cries of “Vincerò!” (“veen-che-RO”)
parts_sanitized: Name this opera that was originally completed by Franco Alfano. In this Puccini opera, Calaf answers three riddles to win the hand of the title Chinese princess.
Puccini left Turandot unfinished after this secondary character's funeral procession. This slave-girl secretly loves Calaf and tearfully begs him not to risk his life in the aria "Signore, ascolta!"
Before Liu's death, Calaf expresses confidence that Turandot won't learn his name in this aria. This aria ends with three cries of "Vincero!" ("veen-che-RO")
answers: “Nessun dorma” [prompt on translations like “No one shall sleep”]

6a2cbf345fef949429e44d67.question: The title character of this opera was James Maddalena’s signature role. At the start of Act III of this opera, a heldentenor muses, “I am no one,” before his coloratura soprano wife gets him to descend from a giant portrait of himself. The title baritone of this opera remarks how “The whole world was listening!” during a handshake in this opera’s Act I aria (*) “News has a kind of mystery.” An “out-take” from this opera is subtitled “Foxtrot for Orchestra.” Act I of this opera begins with the landing of the aircraft Spirit of ’76, and Act II ends with an interrupted performance of The Red Detachment of Women. For 10 points, name this 1987 John Adams opera about a US president’s visit to a country led by Chairman Mao.
6a2cbf345fef949429e44d67.question_sanitized: The title character of this opera was James Maddalena's signature role. At the start of Act III of this opera, a heldentenor muses, "I am no one," before his coloratura soprano wife gets him to descend from a giant portrait of himself. The title baritone of this opera remarks how "The whole world was listening!" during a handshake in this opera's Act I aria (*) "News has a kind of mystery." An "out-take" from this opera is subtitled "Foxtrot for Orchestra." Act I of this opera begins with the landing of the aircraft Spirit of '76, and Act II ends with an interrupted performance of The Red Detachment of Women. For 10 points, name this 1987 John Adams opera about a US president's visit to a country led by Chairman Mao.
6a2cbf345fef949429e44d67.answer: Nixon in China (The “foxtrot for orchestra” is Adams’s The Chairman Dances.)
6a2cbf345fef949429e44d67.answer_sanitized: Nixon in China (The "foxtrot for orchestra" is Adams's The Chairman Dances.)

6a2cbf355fef949429e44dbb.question: In 2026, Lise Davidsen (“Lisa DAH-vid-sen”) made her much-awaited debut in this role, who ends the opera as she “sinks gently, as if transfigured” onto her lover’s dead body as the orchestra holds a B major chord. In her Act I “Narrative and Curse,” this character enlists her maid Brangäne (“bron-GAY-nuh”) to prepare a poison. This woman remarks how “softly and gently” her dead lover shines at the start of a scene in which a chord spelled as (*) “F, B, D-sharp, and G-sharp” is finally resolved in her “love-death,” or “Liebestod” (“LEE-bus-toht”). In a pioneering 1865 opera, this woman is engaged to King Marke but falls in love with a heldentenor after drinking a love potion. For 10 points, name this Irish princess in a Wagner opera who loves the Cornish knight Tristan.
6a2cbf355fef949429e44dbb.question_sanitized: In 2026, Lise Davidsen ("Lisa DAH-vid-sen") made her much-awaited debut in this role, who ends the opera as she "sinks gently, as if transfigured" onto her lover's dead body as the orchestra holds a B major chord. In her Act I "Narrative and Curse," this character enlists her maid Brangane ("bron-GAY-nuh") to prepare a poison. This woman remarks how "softly and gently" her dead lover shines at the start of a scene in which a chord spelled as (*) "F, B, D-sharp, and G-sharp" is finally resolved in her "love-death," or "Liebestod" ("LEE-bus-toht"). In a pioneering 1865 opera, this woman is engaged to King Marke but falls in love with a heldentenor after drinking a love potion. For 10 points, name this Irish princess in a Wagner opera who loves the Cornish knight Tristan.
6a2cbf355fef949429e44dbb.answer: Isolde(“ee-ZOHL-duh”) [or Iseult; prompt on Tristan and Isolde or Tristan und Isolde before “Tristan” is read and accept afterwards; reject “Tristan”]
6a2cbf355fef949429e44dbb.answer_sanitized: Isolde("ee-ZOHL-duh") [or Iseult; prompt on Tristan and Isolde or Tristan und Isolde before "Tristan" is read and accept afterwards; reject "Tristan"]

parts: Name this A-B-A structure common in arias from opera seria. Christoph Willibald Gluck (“glook”) abandoned this type of aria in part of his efforts to “reform” opera.

In the repeat of the A section of a da capo aria, the singer traditionally composes ornamentation in this manner to embellish the melody. This action is when a musician spontaneously composes music as they play.

This Baroque composer wrote the da capo aria “Va tacito e nascosto” (“vah TAH-chee-toh e nas-COS-toh”) for his opera Giulio Cesare(“JOOL-yoh CHEH-zah-ray”). This composer’s aria “Ombra mai fu” is also known as his “Largo from Xerxes.”
parts_sanitized: Name this A-B-A structure common in arias from opera seria. Christoph Willibald Gluck ("glook") abandoned this type of aria in part of his efforts to "reform" opera.

In the repeat of the A section of a da capo aria, the singer traditionally composes ornamentation in this manner to embellish the melody. This action is when a musician spontaneously composes music as they play.

This Baroque composer wrote the da capo aria "Va tacito e nascosto" ("vah TAH-chee-toh e nas-COS-toh") for his opera Giulio Cesare("JOOL-yoh CHEH-zah-ray"). This composer's aria "Ombra mai fu" is also known as his "Largo from Xerxes."
answers_sanitized: improvisation [accept word forms such as improvising]

6a2cbf375fef949429e44e96.question: Francesca Zambello’s 2006 Royal Opera House production of an opera by this composer starred Anna Caterina Antonacci and Jonas Kaufmann (“YO-nahss KOWF-mahn”). This composer wrote the aria “Je crois entendre encore” (“je CRWAHZ on-TON-dron-COR”), in which a tenor reminisces about meeting a veiled woman. In another tenor aria by this composer, an officer recalls cherishing a “withered” object while in prison. This composer wrote the friendship duet (*) “Au fond du temple saint” (“oh FON du TOM-pluh SAHN”) for an opera set in Ceylon. The “Flower Song” is contained in an opera by this composer in which Escamillo’s flirtation with the title woman makes Don José (“zho-ZAY”) jealous. For 10 points, name this composer of The Pearl Fishers who wrote the “Toreador Song” and “Habanera” for Carmen.
6a2cbf375fef949429e44e96.question_sanitized: Francesca Zambello's 2006 Royal Opera House production of an opera by this composer starred Anna Caterina Antonacci and Jonas Kaufmann ("YO-nahss KOWF-mahn"). This composer wrote the aria "Je crois entendre encore" ("je CRWAHZ on-TON-dron-COR"), in which a tenor reminisces about meeting a veiled woman. In another tenor aria by this composer, an officer recalls cherishing a "withered" object while in prison. This composer wrote the friendship duet (*) "Au fond du temple saint" ("oh FON du TOM-pluh SAHN") for an opera set in Ceylon. The "Flower Song" is contained in an opera by this composer in which Escamillo's flirtation with the title woman makes Don Jose ("zho-ZAY") jealous. For 10 points, name this composer of The Pearl Fishers who wrote the "Toreador Song" and "Habanera" for Carmen.
6a2cbf375fef949429e44e96.answer_sanitized: Georges Bizet [or Alexandre Cesar Leopold Bizet]
